steps.title: Identify parallel lines and transversals
steps.content:
Three parallel lines $\overline{AF}$, $\overline{BE}$, and $\overline{CD}$ are cut by two transversals.

The left transversal passes through points A, B, C (on $\overline{AC}$) and the right transversal passes through points F, E, D (on $\overline{FD}$).

When parallel lines cut two transversals, they divide the transversals into proportional segments.

steps.title: Set up the proportion
steps.content:
By the Basic Proportionality Theorem, the segments on one transversal are proportional to corresponding segments on the other transversal:

$$\frac{AB}{BC} = \frac{FE}{ED}$$

Substituting the known values: AB = 9, BC = 18.5, and FE = 8.5:

$$\frac{9}{18.5} = \frac{8.5}{ED}$$

steps.title: Solve for ED
steps.content:
Cross-multiply:

$$9 \cdot ED = 18.5 \cdot 8.5$$

$$9 \cdot ED = 157.25$$

$$ED = \frac{157.25}{9} = 17.472\ldots$$

Rounding to the nearest tenth gives $ED = 17.5$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
